title: A Streetcar Named Desire
creator: Tennessee Williams
description: A fragile woman arrives in New Orleans and disrupts her sister’s marriage.
whyForActors: Blanche DuBois is one of the most frequently performed roles in modern theatre.

title: Hamlet
creator: William Shakespeare
description: A prince returns home to grief, betrayal, and revenge.
whyForActors: The role of Hamlet is one of the longest in the Shakespeare canon.

title: The Seagull
creator: Anton Chekhov
description: Artists, lovers, and families gather at a country estate.
whyForActors: Characters frequently speak indirectly rather than stating intentions clearly.

title: The Cherry Orchard
creator: Anton Chekhov
description: A family estate is sold as social change takes hold.
whyForActors: Chekhov described the play as a comedy despite its themes of loss.

title: Death of a Salesman
creator: Arthur Miller
description: A salesman reflects on his life and expectations.
whyForActors: The play moves between present action and memory sequences.

title: Long Day’s Journey Into Night
creator: Eugene O’Neill
description: A family confronts addiction and the past over a single day.
whyForActors: The entire play unfolds across one continuous day.

title: Who’s Afraid of Virginia Woolf?
creator: Edward Albee
description: Two couples meet late at night and continue into the early hours.
whyForActors: The action takes place in real time over one night.

title: Top Girls
creator: Caryl Churchill
description: A woman’s career intersects with historical and social perspectives.
whyForActors: The opening scene includes characters from different time periods.

title: The Homecoming
creator: Harold Pinter
description: A man returns home with his wife to his family.
whyForActors: Pinter’s writing is known for pauses and silence.

title: A Doll’s House
creator: Henrik Ibsen
description: A woman questions her place in her marriage.
whyForActors: The final scene has been widely discussed and reinterpreted.

title: The Glass Menagerie
creator: Tennessee Williams
description: A memory play about a family in St. Louis.
whyForActors: The narrator is also part of the action.

title: Waiting for Godot
creator: Samuel Beckett
description: Two figures wait for someone who never arrives.
whyForActors: The structure repeats similar events across both acts.

title: The Crucible
creator: Arthur Miller
description: Accusations spread through a Puritan community.
whyForActors: Often staged as an allegory for political persecution.

title: Angels in America
creator: Tony Kushner
description: Multiple characters navigate identity and politics.
whyForActors: Actors often play multiple roles.

title: Jerusalem
creator: Jez Butterworth
description: A man lives on the edge of a rural community.
whyForActors: The central character appears in most scenes.

title: People, Places & Things
creator: Duncan Macmillan
description: An actor enters rehab and confronts addiction.
whyForActors: Explores performance within everyday life.

title: Constellations
creator: Nick Payne
description: A relationship unfolds across multiple possibilities.
whyForActors: Scenes are repeated with variations.

title: The Effect
creator: Lucy Prebble
description: Participants in a trial form a relationship.
whyForActors: Questions the source of emotion.

title: Yerma
creator: Federico García Lorca
description: A woman’s desire for a child shapes her life.
whyForActors: The language is highly poetic and symbolic.

title: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern Are Dead
creator: Tom Stoppard
description: Two minor characters from Hamlet take centre stage.
whyForActors: Reframes events from another play.

title: The History Boys
creator: Alan Bennett
description: Students prepare for university entrance exams.
whyForActors: Features an ensemble cast of students and teachers.

title: One Man, Two Guvnors
creator: Richard Bean
description: A man attempts to serve two employers at once.
whyForActors: Includes elements of physical comedy and audience interaction.

title: The Nightingale and the Rose
creator: Oscar Wilde
description: A story of love, sacrifice, and misunderstanding.
whyForActors: Often adapted into different theatrical forms.

title: Blood Wedding
creator: Federico García Lorca
description: A wedding leads to conflict and tragedy.
whyForActors: Combines realism with symbolic elements.

title: The Maids
creator: Jean Genet
description: Two maids role-play scenarios involving their employer.
whyForActors: Explores identity through role-playing.

title: Hedda Gabler
creator: Henrik Ibsen
description: A woman navigates power and control within her marriage.
whyForActors: The title role is central to most scenes.

title: Miss Julie
creator: August Strindberg
description: A relationship develops across class boundaries.
whyForActors: Often performed with a small cast.

title: The Importance of Being Earnest
creator: Oscar Wilde
description: Two men create false identities.
whyForActors: Known for its use of wit and language.

title: Arcadia
creator: Tom Stoppard
description: Two timelines intersect in the same house.
whyForActors: Moves between past and present scenes.

title: Blasted
creator: Sarah Kane
description: A hotel room setting expands into a wider conflict.
whyForActors: The play shifts dramatically in tone and scale.
